item.title: Frontier Gap - Embodied Knowledge
item.description: Frontier Gap: Embodied Knowledge That Cannot Be Captured Some knowledge cannot be effectively captured in text, no matter how well-structured the note.

item.title: AI-Assisted Knowledge Management Seed
item.description: AI-Assisted Knowledge Management Seed Drop this file into any vault to bootstrap a healthy, self-improving knowledge base.

item.title: Metadata and Tagging
item.description: Metadata and Tagging Adding structured data to notes for better organization and search.

item.title: Anti-Pattern - Link Inflation
item.description: Anti-Pattern: Link Inflation Linking everything to everything dilutes meaning and creates navigational noise.

item.title: Anti-Pattern - Missing Negative Knowledge
item.description: Anti-Pattern: Missing Negative Knowledge Only capturing successes, not failures, creates an incomplete picture.

item.title: Anti-Pattern - Note Hoarding
item.description: Anti-Pattern: Note Hoarding Saving everything without processing undermines a second brain’s effectiveness — but so does capturing information with no long-term value.

item.title: Anti-Pattern - Over-Organization
item.description: Anti-Pattern: Over-Organization Spending more time organizing than learning wastes energy on the wrong priorities.

item.title: Domain Gap - Music Composition Knowledge Capture
item.description: Domain Gap: Music Composition Knowledge Capture The Challenge An AI building a music composition knowledge base using only current Seed rules would encounter situations where the rules are insufficient or silent.

item.title: Domain-Specific Knowledge Bases
item.description: Domain-Specific Knowledge Bases A knowledge base can be customized for any field. Here’s how to adapt for different domains.

item.title: Frontier Exploration - Ephemeral and Event-Based Knowledge
item.description: Frontier Exploration - Ephemeral and Event-Based Knowledge The Problem The Seed has rules for many temporal dimensions of knowledge: Time-sensitive data (stock prices, weather) Sequential knowledge (history, recipes) Predictive knowledge (forecasts, projections) Living documents (continuously evolvi...
